title: Pittosporum lanceolatum
intro: Pittosporum lanceolatum, also known as New Zealand Pittosporum or Kōhia, is a small to medium-sized tree native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Pittosporaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Māori medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of P. lanceolatum include pittosporine, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with P. lanceolatum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
